body:
## Summary

Resolves #166 — moves the logout (sign-out) button to the top-right of the topbar, directly next to the authenticated account handle, as shown in the issue screenshot.

Previously the sign-out control lived inside the collapsible left-hand sidebar popover, so it was only reachable after expanding the menu. It now sits permanently in the top-right corner beside the account handle whenever the user is signed in.

## Changes

- **`KefineTopbar.svelte`**
  - Added a sign-out icon button inside a new `kefine-topbar-account` wrapper that groups the account handle and the sign-out button in the top-right. It renders only when authenticated and an `onSignOut` handler is provided (`data-testid="kefine-topbar-sign-out"`).
  - Removed the sign-out entry from the sidebar utility toolbar (the button was relocated, not duplicated).
  - Added matching styles consistent with the existing auth button (hover, scrolled states).
  - **Mobile fix:** constrained the topbar row to the viewport on narrow screens so its flex children shrink instead of overflowing. The row is a grid item with the default `min-width: auto`, so it was expanding past the viewport and pushing the new account group (and therefore the sign-out button) off the right edge. Now the search shell absorbs the shrinking while the account group keeps its content size, so the sign-out button is always within the viewport and tappable.
- **`+layout.svelte` / `[handle=at_handle]/+page.svelte`** — pass `signOutLabel={localeText.profile.signOut}` so the relocated button is properly labelled in those topbar usages (these also clear two pre-existing `svelte-check` "missing required prop" errors).
- **`e2e/auth.spec.ts`** — new test: after sign-in the top-right sign-out button is visible, and clicking it ends the session (returns to home, button disappears, auth button reverts to "Sign in").

## How to verify / reproduce

1. Sign in (e.g. private-key auth).
2. The sign-out (exit) icon button now appears in the top-right, immediately to the right of the account handle.
3. Clicking it signs out and returns to the home screen.

Automated coverage: `pnpm test:e2e -- -g "sign-out button that ends the session"` — **passes on both `chromium` and `mobile-chromium`.**

## Screenshots (after)

Desktop:

![after-desktop](https://github.com/lefinepro/kefine/blob/issue-166-3ff80d37f99b/docs/screenshots/topbar-signout-after.png?raw=true)

Mobile (Pixel 5 — sign-out/exit icon at far top-right stays within the viewport):

![after-mobile](https://github.com/lefinepro/kefine/blob/issue-166-3ff80d37f99b/docs/screenshots/topbar-signout-mobile-after.png?raw=true)

## Checks

- `pnpm lint` — 0 errors
- `pnpm check` — no new errors (only pre-existing, unrelated ones remain; the Type Check CI job tolerates these by design)
- `pnpm build` — succeeds
- New e2e test passes on both projects

### Note on the E2E job

The remaining E2E failures are **pre-existing** and not introduced by this PR. They were caused by #168 ("comment out onboarding", "require auth before task launch"), which is the current `release` HEAD (commit `5893882`). Evidence:

- The CI run on `release` at `5893882` (run `27292145950`) already fails **E2E Tests** with the same set of tests, before this PR exists.
- A full local E2E run of this branch was diffed against that release run: **every test that fails here also fails on `release`** — this PR adds **zero** new failures, and the one new test it adds (the sign-out test) passes on both projects.

Fixing that test debt means migrating ~30 tests to the post-#168 flow (no onboarding screen, auth required before task creation), which is out of scope for "move the logout button" and belongs with #168. Happy to take it on in a follow-up if desired.

Fixes #166
title: Move the logout button to the top-right topbar
